name: Operations/member-match
description: $member-match operation conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E.Rest.MemberMatchTests and MemberMatchTestFixture. $member-match (MemberMatchController) takes a Parameters resource with 'MemberPatient' and 'OldCoverage' resources and performs a live FHIR search across all Patient/Coverage data already on the target server (MemberMatchService.CreateSearchExpression indexes every searchable field present in the two input resources, chained through Coverage.beneficiary) to find exactly one matching, previously-known member; 0 matches or 2+ matches both raise a 422 UnprocessableEntity. Because this engine only supports static fixture bodies (no dynamic templating to correlate a freshly-created Patient's server-assigned id into a Coverage body) and because the match outcome is fundamentally sensitive to whatever other data already exists on the target, most of this suite is necessarily shape/status-code checking rather than a deterministic outcome check. One test does set up a self-consistent, distinctively-tagged Patient+Coverage pair via a single transaction Bundle (fullUrl urn:uuid cross-reference, matching the pattern already used in Bundles/transaction.json) to make a real 200 match plausible, but this still depends on transaction-Bundle support, $member-match support, and the absence of any other data matching the same distinctive values, so it remains warningOnly.

variable.name: memberMatchSetupPatientId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the distinctively-tagged Patient created for the member-match setup test, used for teardown

variable.name: memberMatchSetupCoverageId
variable.description: Server-assigned id of the Coverage created for the member-match setup test, used for teardown

test.name: $member-match rejects a non-Parameters request body with 400 Bad Request
test.description: Ported from MemberMatchTests.GivenNonParametersRequestBody_WhenMemberMatchSent_ThenBadRequest: POST Patient/$member-match with a plain Patient body (not wrapped in Parameters) must return 400. Unlike $reindex/$convert-data, $member-match has no enabled/disabled configuration flag in this codebase, so this is the most reliable assertion in this suite; still warningOnly because a non-Microsoft-FHIR-Server target may not implement $member-match at all (404 instead of 400).

operation.description: POST Patient/$member-match with a bare Patient body

assert.description: Server should reject a non-Parameters body with 400 Bad Request (warningOnly)

test.name: $member-match request/response shape with unseeded data
test.description: POST Patient/$member-match with a well-formed Parameters(MemberPatient, OldCoverage) request built from data that is very unlikely to already exist on the target (per MemberMatchTests.GivenUniqueInformation_WhenMemberMatchSent_ThenPatientFound / GivenNotEnoughInformation.../GivenTooMuchInformation... patterns, which rely on server-seeded fixture data this engine cannot reproduce). The realistic outcomes are 422 UnprocessableEntity (no match found, MemberMatchService throws MemberMatchNoMatchFound) or 200 (if this exact shape coincidentally matches existing target data). Both assertions are informational/warningOnly since the true outcome depends entirely on pre-existing target data.

operation.description: POST Patient/$member-match with a plausible but unseeded MemberPatient/OldCoverage pair

assert.description: Most likely on a target with no matching seed data: 422 Unprocessable Entity, no match found (warningOnly, informational)
Response should be an OperationOutcome when no/multiple match is reported (warningOnly, informational)

test.name: $member-match finds a self-seeded, distinctively-tagged Patient via its matching Coverage
test.description: Creates one distinctively-tagged Patient (city='IgnixaMemberMatchCityXYZ', family='IgnixaMemberMatchFamilyXYZ', a Medicare-Beneficiary-type identifier) and one Coverage referencing it (type code='IGNIXAMMTESTXYZ') via a single transaction Bundle with a urn:uuid cross-reference, then immediately member-matches on those same distinctive values. If both transaction Bundles and $member-match are supported, and nothing else on the target coincidentally shares these distinctive values, this should return 200 with the matched Patient (MemberMatchService.CreatePatientWithIdentity copies the 'MB' identifier onto a new 'UMB'-typed identifier in the response). warningOnly throughout because it is a compound dependency (transaction Bundle + $member-match + no data collisions).

operation.description: POST transaction Bundle creating the distinctively-tagged Patient + Coverage

assert.description: Setup transaction should return 200 with both entries created (warningOnly: depends on transaction Bundle support)

operation.description: POST Patient/$member-match for the just-created distinctively-tagged Patient/Coverage

assert.description: Should find exactly one match and return 200 OK (warningOnly)
Response should be a Parameters resource containing the matched MemberPatient (warningOnly)
Matched Patient should carry a 'UMB'-typed identifier copied from the seeded 'MB' identifier (warningOnly)

operation.description: Teardown: delete the Coverage created for the member-match setup test
Teardown: delete the Patient created for the member-match setup test
